title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host IPC namespace

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host IPC namespace
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the hostIPC flag set to true.
input_row.rationale: A container running in the host's IPC namespace can use IPC to interact with processes outside the container. There should be at least one admission control policy defined which does not permit containers to share the host IPC namespace. If you need to run containers which require hostIPC, this should be definited in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods defined with spec.hostIPC: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.
input_row.audit: To fetch hostIPC from each pod. get pods -A -o=jsonpath=$'{range .items[*]}{@.metadata.name}: {@.spec.hostIPC}\n{end}'
input_row.remediation: Add policies to each namespace in the cluster which has user workloads to restrict the admission of hostIPC containers. Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of hostIPC containers. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/
input_row.impact: Pods defined with spec.hostIPC: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_host_ipc_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all namespaces in the cluster.
- For each namespace, check for the presence and enforcement of Pod Security Admission (PSA) policies or PodSecurityPolicies (if legacy PSP is still in use) that restrict the use of `hostIPC: true`.
- Use the Kubernetes API or `kubectl` to:
  - List all pods in all namespaces:  
    `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json`
  - For each pod, inspect the `spec.hostIPC` field. Flag any pod where `spec.hostIPC: true`.
  - Additionally, check for PodSecurity or PodSecurityPolicy resources:
    - For PSA:  
      `kubectl get ns --show-labels`  
      Ensure namespaces have at least `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline` or `restricted` (these levels disallow `hostIPC: true`).
    - For PSP (if enabled):  
      `kubectl get psp -o yaml`  
      Ensure policies with `hostIPC: true` are not allowed except for tightly scoped exceptions.
- Pass Criteria:
  - No pods in user namespaces have `spec.hostIPC: true`.
  - All user namespaces enforce Pod Security Admission at least at the `baseline` level, or equivalent PodSecurityPolicy is in place.
- Fail Criteria:
  - Any pod in a user namespace has `spec.hostIPC: true`, or
  - Any user namespace lacks a policy preventing `hostIPC: true` except for narrowly scoped exceptions.
- This check can be fully automated via Kubernetes API queries and does not require subjective review.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.10 PDF
